questions.context:
The table shows the times (in minutes) taken by 80 students to complete a puzzle.

| Time (min) | Frequency | Cumulative Frequency |
|------------|-----------|----------------------|
| 0 < t ≤ 10 | 8         |                      |
| 10 < t ≤ 20| 16        |                      |
| 20 < t ≤ 30| 24        |                      |
| 30 < t ≤ 40| 20        |                      |
| 40 < t ≤ 50| 12        |                      |

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Using the cumulative frequency diagram (plot points at upper class boundaries), find the median time.
workingSteps: From graph: read across at cf = 40 to t-axis

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Find the lower quartile, upper quartile and interquartile range.

markScheme.desc: Q1 at cf = 20, Q3 at cf = 60

parts.label: d
parts.questionText: Find the number of students who took more than 35 minutes.

markScheme.desc: Reads cf at t = 35 from graph: cf ≈ 58; then 80 − 58

parts.label: e
parts.questionText:
The puzzle is considered 'fast' if completed in under 25 minutes.
Estimate the percentage of students who completed it 'fast'.

markScheme.desc: Reads cf at t = 25 from graph: cf ≈ 36; then 36/80 × 100

questions.questionText:
Two classes, A and B, each with 60 students, sat the same test. Their results are displayed as cumulative frequency diagrams.

Class A: median = 52, IQR = 18
Class B: median = 58, IQR = 30

(a) Which class performed better overall? Give a reason.
(b) Which class had more consistent results? Give a reason.
questions.correctAnswer: (a) Class B, higher median. (b) Class A, smaller IQR.

markScheme.desc: (a) Class B has a higher median score oe
(b) Class A has a smaller IQR / less spread in the middle 50% oe

workingSteps: (a) Median measures the 'typical' score; Class B median 58 > Class A median 52
(b) IQR measures consistency; Class A IQR 18 < Class B IQR 30 → Class A more consistent
questions.explanation: Median compares typical performance. IQR compares consistency (smaller = more consistent).

questions.questionText:
A cumulative frequency diagram for the masses of 100 parcels shows the following readings:
- At 2 kg: cumulative frequency = 25
- At 4 kg: cumulative frequency = 50
- At 6 kg: cumulative frequency = 75

(a) Show that the median mass is 4 kg.
(b) Write down the lower quartile and upper quartile.
(c) Find the interquartile range.

markScheme.desc: (b) Q1 = 2 kg (at cf = 25), Q3 = 6 kg (at cf = 75)

workingSteps: (a) Median at cf = 100/2 = 50; at cf = 50, mass = 4 kg ✓
questions.explanation: Median: cf = n/2. Q1: cf = n/4. Q3: cf = 3n/4. IQR = Q3 − Q1.
